item.title: So...
item.content:encoded:
Does this mean other sites like this are floating around?

*punches in www.freemilitary.███*

In all seriousness, I kinda want more. This seems like an MCFey thing to do.

item.title: Licensing JS Post
item.content:encoded: Image used is non-compliant due to requiring a payed license. As such, the image has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So short, so simple. Just take my +1
Does that mean the website is of no practical use now? I guess when it is fully developed, it will save numerous lives.. I even feel sorry for Foundation because it must hide the anomaly away.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-1584 does not waterproof the objects it is loaded onto;

oh! the irony

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What if I open it on my laptop and put a large zipbag over it?
Does it still work?

item.title: One of the reasons I love this
item.content:encoded: One of the reasons this is a good SCP is because it doesn't waterproof the device. Thank you so much. A lot of writers like to make their objects "invincible" for many reasons, and in my opinion, it seems like lazy writing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: maybe when SCPReadings read it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Holy cow, when did this article get so many votes

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love it. Great idea, and executed well. Just a question:

Why is it Euclid? Is it because it is a webpage that seems to be unprotectable and easily accessed by the general public?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like it. It's bizarre, keeps its theme consistant short and to the point and tells you just enough to be intrigued while leaving the details to the reader's imagination.

I also really like the attention to little details like specifying what happens on small devices or specifying that the original blog post was so obscure that it wasn't a security risk.

Very good example of a non-horror SCP. Happily upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So, is this based on the joke end user agreement "this device shall not be used as a flotation device", right?

item.title: +200/ thanks, OS
item.content:encoded: This was really fun.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In light (lol) of the Short Works Contest, I'd like to redraw attention to this work as an example of a superb short-form SCP. If you plan on entering the contest, definitely read this one over a few times.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's some weird-ass programming. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this skip, short and sweet. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
flotationdevice.com

the idea is epic

but who tries to program a flotation device???

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Huh. Interesting. I like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think my favorite part about this is the fact that this is only #4 on the Top Weirdest Technology Websites list. What the hell is 1-3?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I bet enough people searched it that it got taken precisely because of this article.
Regardless, upvoted with no regrets. Great first SCP, reminiscent of the much more minimalist style of the olden times when I was also not around.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wishun, how do you do such good interpretations? Every time I see you posting your own interpretations of the backstory it makes complete and perfect sense in some way.

item.title: Re: Welcome to SCP Airlines
item.content:encoded: Cross test, anyone? *demoted*

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not psychic. Just familiar with the ways of domain squatters.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
WEEEOOO WEEEOOO CODE RED CODE RED

Apparently www.floatationdevice.net is now actually in use. Voct is a psychic. This leaves me with three options:

  1. Pretend that this website is a Foundation cover (sort of icky)
  2. Switch to a slightly different domain name (unreliable)
  3. Redact the TLD, as Voct suggested (but I don't wanna)

For now, I'll go with option three. In the beginning, I wanted the domain to be floatationdevice.com, anyways.

Fun fact: I realized this while reading the Russian comments for my SCP. Thank you, "4opHbIu_6o6p4opHbIu_6o6p". Also, I may or may not have to fix something about "molecular density".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wait, seriously? What would you use it for?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I would very much like it if this actually existed IRL… I would use it so much. Somebody make it, why is no one working on it yet?

item.title: Re: +100
item.content:encoded: Holy frickin' shit, man. Well done!

item.title: Re: +100
item.content:encoded:
SELLOUT!

=P

item.title: Re: +100
item.content:encoded: This is incredible. There's not really anything I can say that won't sound either arrogant or like false humility, so just thanks, everybody.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The style is so classic it's almost retro. Nicely done.

+1
Your first SCP and it's already at 100, good job, you deserved it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really enjoy this because it's short and because it's not actually clear what is causing the anomaly. I like that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not sure whether I like or dislike the short length, but overall it was pretty good. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It was a toss up for me. It's very powerful, considering that it can remotely transmute objects. Also, although currently it seems contained, there's nothing stopping the developer from opening up a new site.

If others disagree I'll definitely change it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wait a sec, this hasn't occurred to me earlier but, why is this Euclid? If left alone all that's gonna happen is a mobile device version. Does hat really grant a Euclid designation?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Stuff like this getting upvoted this much is a good sign for the future. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is probably the best new SCP i have seen in a while. Short, sweet, perfect. Good work. Please, stick around.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this interpretation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It'd make far more sense to simply weigh the device, since the report states that the skip causes things to lose mass but that they don't become waterproof.

+1, btw. Great idea, well executed plus you got grammar people debating correct terms.

item.title: Re: Am I missing something?
item.content:encoded:
You're giving too much of the credit to it being your first. I evaluted it purely on the merits of the concept and writing. I didn't learn it was your first until after voting. I believe most people will have similar, and not evaluated it based on the author or previous work (or lack there-of).

In short: Ya done good. Take the compliment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is incredibly accurate. It sounds like several people i know, and matches most programming environments perfectly. You can bet that he has a whole set of bugs that his beta testers submitted back to him that he replied to with "Problem with hardware, not software. Closed."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I sea what you did there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I get a grin out of thinking about the guy developing this.
A fairly normal programming geek, possibly just a hair more out of touch with reality than your stereotypical computer nerd. He set out to create a useful service, something that might even save a few lives. The beta worked pretty well. True, there were some hardware issues. But it's hardly his fault if the users don't even properly epoxy seal their computer cases!
N-dimensional mass displacement via packet transfer was pretty straightforward, the hard part was integrating GPS data with a database of local rescue services.
Then, just as he was this close to working the kinks out of the mobile app, some stupid Men In Black DDOS his site! For no good reason, WTF!

item.title: Re: Am I missing something?
item.content:encoded:
Ah, then never mind my wish then. I apparently forgot completely about the tomato SCP and thought humor was regulated to the -js. I think I'll give it an upvote now.

Although, 1867 is skirting the edge a bit. It's sillier and funnier than some of the weaker
-js.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes! Yes he can. This was a conscious decision I made. I looked for domains until I got one that was unused. Redacting just wouldn't feel… right.

item.title: Re: Am I missing something?
item.content:encoded: Yeah, I was responding to Eregorn

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, if that happens, the author can pick a different one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What concerns me is our use of an actual URL. What will happen if, someday, someone buys that domain and hosts stuff on it?

I strongly suggest redacting the TLD.

item.title: Re: Am I missing something?
item.content:encoded: Yeah, I definitely agree with that. With my post, I wasn't really trying to imply that being short was a problem. I was really more trying to say that it is able to accomplish a lot with very little.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A model for how to write short scps!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is good and you should feel good about this.

item.title: Re: Am I missing something?
item.content:encoded:
I sort of want this as a joke SCP

It won't work as one

this one works despite it's length

A lot of the reason this article works is because of its length, it's more or less a callback to the simpler SCPs that take up many of the 1st series.
But more importantly, it's a lighthearted kind of article that doesn't go for extra addendums or interviews to gain the reader's attention, but instead
has several subtle jokes to amuse the reader.

Going back to your Joke statement:
There are several funny SCPs in existence that aren't jokes because they simply aren't meant to be, same with this.
for instance, compare these mainlist SCPs:
http://www.scp-wiki.net/scp-504
http://www.scp-wiki.net/scp-1794
http://www.scp-wiki.net/scp-586
http://www.scp-wiki.net/scp-1867
to these jokes:
http://www.scp-wiki.net/scp-789-j
http://www.scp-wiki.net/scp-065-j
http://www.scp-wiki.net/scp-666-j
http://www.scp-wiki.net/scp-420-j
You will notice a definite difference in silliness between these, one is pretty silly, but the other takes it to a whole new extreme.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yep, that sounds better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Buoy, who do you think you are

item.title: Re: Am I missing something?
item.content:encoded:
I mean… I don't think so.

I just had a idea, decided to join the site, and wrote it from a certain perspective. Why it's popular, though, I'm not really entitled to say. The fact that this is my first SCP is probably influencing people.

I think an SCP can be a joke without being a joke SCP. It's sort of the difference between standup comedy and deadpan. SCP-1286 is a great example.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My only suggestion is changing "unpopularity" to "lack of popularity."

+1'd.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It might even help keep the site afloat

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is so good. Gives me good feelings for the future.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Er, I meant that the website is still accessible and the servers haven't been located or shut down. Sorry, my bad there.

item.title: Re: Am I missing something?
item.content:encoded:
I think a lot of the reason why this one works despite it's length is that I feel that there is a lot of implied story. It makes me wonder who would make something like this, and why. It's features, such as not waterproofing the devices and an "upcoming" mobile version do a good job of hinting at the answers. So, I think there is more there, but it's just hidden between the lines.

Plus, it's just a creative idea for an anomalous effect.

item.title: Am I missing something?
item.content:encoded:
A serious question, considering this is almost at 100+. All I'm seeing here is a webpage that makes your computer a floatation device and calls emergency services. Admittedly, I guess I could understand wanting something simple considering how SCPs fail more often than not by droning on and on. However, I personally don't think there's enough for thought, although might be because I could practically use this if it worked.

On that note, I sort of want this as a joke SCP as strange as it sounds. Sure, its not literally lol funny, but it's a bit punny, got a smirk when it tells you it doesn't really work either due to water damage, and enjoyed the little quip on the web blog.
